title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make with just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. This traditional recipe uses a salt-curing method where lemons ferment for four weeks, creating a flavorful ingredient with a citrusy, umami-rich taste.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons, and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters, without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Start packing the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible so lemon juice is released.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice, adding juice from an extra lemon if needed.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks, or up to a year for darker lemons.
Once opened, store in the fridge indefinitely, rinsing off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes.
